figures.caption: Figure 5: Mean and standard deviation of the offline error for DCC-MKP instances with a fixed item-to-knapsack ratio n/m = 10 and increasing problem sizes (n,m) ∈ {(100, 10), (300, 30), (500, 50)}. Results are reported under dynamic capacity changes ν ∈ {20, 50, 100} for strongly correlated and uncorrelated instances with η = 0.2.

Figure 6: Mean and standard deviation of the offline error for FK4 DCC-MKP instances with item-to-knapsack ratios n/m ∈ {2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10}. Results are shown under dynamic capacity changes ν ∈ {20, 50, 100} for strongly correlated and uncorrelated instances with η = 0.2.

Figure 1: Mean and standard deviation of the best obtained profits for CC-MKP instances with a fixed number of items (n = 300) and varying numbers of knapsacks (m ∈ {150, 50, 30}). Results are shown for variance levels v ∈ {v1, v2} across different confidence levels α.

Figure 2: Mean and standard deviation of the best obtained profits for CC-MKP instances with a fixed item-to-knapsack ratio n/m = 10 and increasing problem sizes (n,m) ∈ {(100, 10), (300, 30), (500, 50)}. Results are presented for variance levels v ∈ {v1, v2} across different confidence levels α.

Figure 3: Mean and standard deviation of the best obtained profits for FK4 CC-MKP instances with varying itemto-knapsack ratios n/m ∈ {2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10}. Results are shown for variance levels v ∈ {v1, v2} and four confidence levels α.

Figure 4: Mean and standard deviation of the offline error for DCC-MKP instances with a fixed number of items (n = 300) and varying numbers of knapsacks (m ∈ {150, 50, 30}), under dynamic capacity changes with frequencies ν ∈ {20, 50, 100}. Results are presented for strongly correlated and uncorrelated instances with change magnitude η = 0.2.
